nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Schoenfeld
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Erwin, Franz, Klaus, Adelheid, Eugen, Frieda, Fritz, Gerhard, Hanns.
German (Schönfeld): habitational name from any of numerous places called Schönfeld, from Middle High German schoen ‘beautiful’ + velt ‘open country, field’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Schönfeld): artificial name composed of German schön ‘beautiful’ + Feld ‘field’. Compare Schonfeld .

variants.name: Shufeldt
meanings: Americanized form of German   Schoenfeld  .

variants.name: Schonfeld
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Chaim, Yoel, Aron, Elihu, Hyman, Mordechi, Naftula, Sholom, Tovia, Zalman.  German Klaus, Erwin, Gunter, Kurt.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (Schönfeld): see Schoenfeld .

variants.name: Shufelt
meanings: Americanized form of German   Schoenfeld  .

variants.name: Shenefelt
meanings: Americanized form of German Schönfeld (see Schoenfeld ).

variants.name: Schoenfeldt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Arno, Kurt.
German (Schönfeldt): variant of Schönfeld (see Schoenfeld ).

variants.name: Shoenfelt
meanings: Americanized form of German Schönfeld (see Schoenfeld ).

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Older
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: status name for an old man or an alderman, chairman of a corporation or a city or church council. See   Old  .
English (southeastern): variant of   Alder  .

variants.name: Old
meanings: variant of   Olaf  , also widely used in southwest Sweden.

en.lifeSummary: When Jan the Older von Schoenfeld was born about 1365, in Naundorf, Weißenfels, Saxony, Germany, his father, Siegfried the Great von Schoenfeld, was 32 and his mother, Mrs. Siegfried von Schoenfeld, was 24. He married Else Naundorf about 1398, in Naundorf, Radebeul, Meißen, Saxony, Germany.
